title: Elvasia sagotii
intro: Elvasia sagotii, also known as Elvasia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Guttiferae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Elvasia sagotii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Elvasia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of tinctures and ointments, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Elvasia sagotii is classified in the family Guttiferae, which is a family of flowering plants. However, it's worth noting that the classification of Guttiferae is now considered outdated. The plant is more accurately classified within the order Malpighiales. The family Guttiferae was merged into the family Clusiaceae, which is a diverse group of plants. Elvasia sagotii is a species of flowering plant within the family Clusiaceae.
